title: Codex Entries by Category
query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total FROM CodexEntry WHERE IsNewEntry = 1 AND event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate
detailQuery: SELECT COALESCE(Category_Localised, Category) AS label, COUNT(*) AS value FROM CodexEntry WHERE IsNewEntry = 1 AND event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate GROUP BY Category ORDER BY value DESC

details.text: {total} new codex entry(ies) recorded during the report period. The Codex is humanity's collective knowledge of the galaxy — each new entry you add is genuinely your own, and the category breakdown reveals whether you're a biologist, stellar cartographer, xenologist, or a generalist explorer touching every knowledge domain.
details.help: CodexEntry IsNewEntry = 1 marks genuine first-discovery codex additions. total drives the title.

tagLines: Codex entry acquisition log compiled. New knowledge domain additions noted in the discovery record.
The Codex grew because of what you found. Each entry is a page you personally added.
Some commanders scan the galaxy. You added to the record of it. That's a different kind of contribution.
